summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) technology in Firefox enhance logging and media handling capabilities. By using **MOZ_LOG_FMT** for logging, developers can better track the performance and issues in video calls. Additionally, the expiration for webrtcdtls.KEA has been updated to keep up with security standards, ensuring safer communications. The browser also now rejects H.264 video requests if they exceed specified resolution or frame rate limits, preventing potential playback issues and optimizing performance. Finally, improvements have been made with a new H.264 FMTP (Format Parameter) parser, which aids in managing video streaming formats more effectively.
